title: machine: respect `--quiet` flag on `podman machine start`
body:
From #28687; 

`podman machine start --quiet` is not respected and ends up printing a success message:

```
$ podman machine start --quiet                                                     
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

Also, It's worth noting if the lack of `--quiet` is intentional in `podman machine stop`, since that also prints the same success message.

comments.body:
I think design wise when this was implemented this was intentional, see the examples in the docs
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-machine-start.1.html

cc @ashley-cui

I think this was the original discussion: https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/16186#issuecomment-1314428225

This behavior is also consistent with other `--quiet` behavior: `podman pull -q` doesn't suppress all output, but rather only quiets everything except the pulled image ID. I'd argue that this is not a bug and is as designed. Closing for now, but if anyone has strong opinions, feel free to re-open and we can continue discussions.

Thanks for sharing that comment. I don't have a strong opinion on this, just that it felt counterintuitive to see some output even with `--quiet`. Though I can see `--noout` works as intended in this case.
